perturbation.original_text: A. CONSULTANT'S "ENDORSEMENT" means the right to use the CONSULTANT'S name, fame, nickname, autograph, voice, facsimile, signature, photograph, likeness, and image in connection with the marketing, advertising, promotion and sale of ADAMS GOLF'S PRODUCT.
perturbation.changed_text: A. CONSULTANT'S "ENDORSEMENT" means the right to use the CONSULTANT'S name, fame, nickname, autograph, voice, facsimile, signature, photograph, likeness, and image in connection with the marketing, advertising, promotion and sale of ADAMS GOLF'S PRODUCT. However, Adams Golf reserves the right to restrict the use of Consultant's endorsement for specific products at its sole discretion.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'ENDORSEMENT' is broad and unqualified. The added sentence allows Adams Golf to arbitrarily restrict the endorsement, contradicting the initially broad definition and creating uncertainty about the scope of the endorsement granted. It contradicts section 3, which grants Adams Golf the *exclusive* right to use the consultant's endorsement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: CONSULTANT hereby gives and grants to ADAMS GOLF the exclusive right and license to use CONSULTANT'S ENDORSEMENT in connection with the manufacture, sale, distribution, advertising and promotion of PRODUCT in the CONTRACT TERRITORY.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'ENDORSEMENT' broadly. The changed text adds a clause allowing Adams Golf to restrict the endorsement's use, which directly contradicts the 'exclusive right' granted to Adams Golf in the contradicted text. The contradicted text explicitly states that Adams Golf has the *exclusive* right to use the consultant's endorsement, whereas the changed text gives them the right to restrict its use for specific products. This creates an inconsistency and uncertainty in the agreement, and therefore a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: During the term of this Agreement, CONSULTANT shall exclusively play/use the MANDATORY PRODUCT. (It is expressly understood by the parties that CONSULTANT may play [* ****] clubs in the bag other than ADAMS GOLF clubs including, but not limited to, a putter by a manufacturer other than ADAMS GOLF but may not endorse those clubs and/or putter.)
perturbation.changed_text: During the term of this Agreement, CONSULTANT shall exclusively play/use the MANDATORY PRODUCT. (It is expressly understood by the parties that CONSULTANT may play [* ****] clubs in the bag other than ADAMS GOLF clubs including, but not limited to, a putter by a manufacturer other than ADAMS GOLF, and in certain approved promotional contexts, may endorse those clubs and/or putter with prior written consent from Adams Golf.)
perturbation.explanation: The original clause explicitly forbids endorsing non-Adams Golf clubs. The added phrase contradicts this by allowing endorsement of non-Adams Golf clubs under certain conditions (approved promotional contexts). This creates an in-text contradiction and uncertainty, violating the original exclusivity agreement and contradicting section 4 where states restrictions on giving the right to use the consultant's name or likeness to other manufacturer or seller of PRODUCT.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the term of this Agreement, unless otherwise authorized at the sole discretion of ADAMS GOLF in writing, CONSULTANT shall not: A.give the right to use or permit the use of CONSULTANT'S name, facsimile signature, nickname, voice or likeness to any other manufacturer or seller of PRODUCT; B.sponsor or endorse PRODUCT made or sold by any other manufacturer or seller; or C.serve as a CONSULTANT or advisor of any other manufacturer or seller of PRODUCT.
perturbation.justification: The original text forbids endorsing non-Adams Golf clubs. The changed text contradicts this by allowing endorsement of non-Adams Golf clubs with prior written consent. The contradicted text states the consultant shall not give the right to use his likeness to any other manufacturer or seller of product, or endorse product made by another manufacturer. The changed text creates a direct contradiction by permitting endorsements of competing products, which is explicitly prohibited by the contradicted section.

perturbation.original_text: TERMINATION 22. FAILURE TO PLAY Notwithstanding any other paragraph of this Agreement, in the event that CONSULTANT dies or is unable to play tour golf at all, ADAMS GOLF may terminate this Agreement on thirty (30) days' written notice. Upon such termination, CONSULTANT and/or CONSULTANT'S estate shall be required to repay ADAMS GOLF for that period of the Agreement not performed by CONSULTANT. The repayment amount shall be calculated in the same way as repayment is calculated under paragraph 8A.
perturbation.changed_text: TERMINATION 22. FAILURE TO PLAY Notwithstanding any other paragraph of this Agreement, in the event that CONSULTANT dies or is unable to play tour golf at all, ADAMS GOLF may terminate this Agreement on thirty (30) days' written notice. Upon such termination, CONSULTANT and/or CONSULTANT'S estate may, at Adams Golf's sole discretion, be required to repay ADAMS GOLF for that period of the Agreement not performed by CONSULTANT. The repayment amount may be calculated in the same way as repayment is calculated under paragraph 8A.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause mandates repayment by the consultant's estate if the agreement is terminated due to the consultant's death or inability to play. By inserting 'may, at Adams Golf's sole discretion' it introduces ambiguity. The phrase "may be calculated in the same way" creates an in-text contradiction and uncertainty, because originally was calculated in one way (same way as repayment under 8A), but know with the changes made, it can also be calculated in another way or it may not, depending on Adams Golf's decision
perturbation.contradicted_text: FAILURE TO PLAY Notwithstanding any other paragraph of this Agreement, in the event that CONSULTANT dies or is unable to play tour golf at all, ADAMS GOLF may terminate this Agreement on thirty (30) days' written notice. Upon such termination, CONSULTANT and/or CONSULTANT'S estate may, at Adams Golf's sole discretion, be required to repay ADAMS GOLF for that period of the Agreement not performed by CONSULTANT. The repayment amount may be calculated in the same way as repayment is calculated under paragraph 8A.
perturbation.justification: The original clause mandated repayment by the consultant's estate. The changed text introduces 'may, at Adams Golf's sole discretion,' creating ambiguity about whether repayment is required at all. It now contradicts itself regarding repayment terms and conditionality, generating an in-text contradiction and uncertainty, because originally repayment was mandatory, but now, with the changes made, it can be optional, depending on Adams Golf's decision. This aligns with the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction, where a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.
